Then, He proceeds to mention His favors, first saying: "I strengthened you with the Holy Spirit" (إِذْ أَيَّدْتُكَ بِرُوحِ الْقُدُسِ‌).

The meaning of the "Holy Spirit" was discussed in detail in Volume 1, page 236. In brief, one possibility is that it refers to the Angel of Revelation, Gabriel, and another possibility is that it refers to that unseen power that strengthened Jesus to perform miracles and fulfill his important mission; this meaning exists in a weaker degree in those other than prophets as well.

Another of the Divine favors upon you is that "by the support of the Holy Spirit, you spoke to the people in the cradle and in maturity" (تُكَلِّمُ النَّاسَ فِي الْمَهْدِ وَ كَهْلًا).

This points to the fact that your speech in the cradle was like your speech in maturity—mature and calculated—not childish and trivial talk.

Another is that "I taught you the Book and wisdom, and the Torah and the Gospel" (وَ إِذْ عَلَّمْتُكَ الْكِتابَ وَ الْحِكْمَةَ وَ التَّوْراةَ وَ الْإِنْجِيلَ‌).

Mentioning the Torah and the Gospel after mentioning "the Book," despite them being among the Heavenly Books, is in fact a case of providing detail after a general statement.

Another favor is that "from clay, by My permission, you would fashion the likeness of a bird, then you would breathe into it, and it would become a living bird by My permission" (وَ إِذْ تَخْلُقُ مِنَ الطِّينِ كَهَيْئَةِ الطَّيْرِ بِإِذْنِي فَتَنْفُخُ فِيها فَتَكُونُ طَيْراً بِإِذْنِي‌).

Another is that: "you would heal the blind and the leper by My permission" (وَ تُبْرِئُ الْأَكْمَهَ وَ الْأَبْرَصَ بِإِذْنِي‌).

And also: "you would bring forth the dead by My permission" (وَ إِذْ تُخْرِجُ الْمَوْتى‌ بِإِذْنِي‌).

And finally, another of My favors upon you was that "I restrained the Children of Israel from harming you when their disbelievers rose up against your clear proofs and characterized them as manifest sorcery." I protected you against all this clamor and your persistent and stubborn enemies so that you could advance your call (وَ إِذْ كَفَفْتُ بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ عَنْكَ إِذْ جِئْتَهُمْ بِالْبَيِّناتِ فَقالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْهُمْ إِنْ هذا إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُبِينٌ‌).

It is noteworthy that in this verse, the phrase "بإذني" (by My permission/command) is repeated four times, so that no room remains for exaggeration (ghuluw) or claims of divinity regarding Prophet Jesus عليه السلام. That is, whatever he performed, although very strange and wonderful and resembling Divine acts, none of it was from his own side; rather, all was performed from Allah's side. He was a servant submissive to Allah's command, and whatever he possessed was through seeking aid from the inexhaustible Divine power.

It might be asked: all these favors pertained to Jesus عليه السلام, so why are they counted as a favor for his mother Mary in this verse?

The answer is that it is certain that any favor reaching a child has, in reality, reached the mother as well, for both are from one origin—the branch and the root of one tree.

Furthermore, as we noted under verse 49 of Surah Al-Imran, this verse and its like are clear proofs of the "Generative Authority" (Wilayah Takwini) of the friends (Awliya) of Allah. This is because in the story of Jesus, the raising of the dead and the healing of the blind and the incurably ill are attributed to the person of Jesus عليه السلام himself, albeit by the permission and command of Allah.

From this expression, it is understood that it is possible for Allah to place such power for exercising authority over the created world at the disposal of someone so that they may occasionally perform such acts. Interpreting this verse as merely the prophets praying and Allah answering their prayers is completely contrary to the apparent meaning of the verses. Our meaning of the "Generative Authority" of the friends of Allah is nothing other than what was mentioned above, as we have no evidence for more than this amount (for further explanation, refer to Volume 2, page 422).

Ayahs

وَإِذْ أَوْحَيْتُ إِلَى ٱلْحَوَارِيِّـۧنَ أَنْ ءَامِنُوا۟ بِى وَبِرَسُولِى قَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا وَٱشْهَدْ بِأَنَّنَا مُسْلِمُونَ (١١١)
And when I inspired the Disciples, [saying], ‘Have faith in Me and My apostle,’ they said, ‘We have faith. Bear witness that we are Muslims.’ (111)
إِذْ قَالَ ٱلْحَوَارِيُّونَ يَـٰعِيسَى ٱبْنَ مَرْيَمَ هَلْ يَسْتَطِيعُ رَبُّكَ أَن يُنَزِّلَ عَلَيْنَا مَآئِدَةً مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ ۖ قَالَ ٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (١١٢)
When the Disciples said, ‘O Jesus son of Mary! Can your Lord send down to us a table from the sky?’ Said he, ‘Be wary of Allah, should you be faithful.’ (112)
قَالُوا۟ نُرِيدُ أَن نَّأْكُلَ مِنْهَا وَتَطْمَئِنَّ قُلُوبُنَا وَنَعْلَمَ أَن قَدْ صَدَقْتَنَا وَنَكُونَ عَلَيْهَا مِنَ ٱلشَّـٰهِدِينَ (١١٣)
They said, ‘We desire to eat from it, and our hearts will be at rest: we shall know that you have told us the truth, and we shall be among the witnesses to it.’ (113)
قَالَ عِيسَى ٱبْنُ مَرْيَمَ ٱللَّهُمَّ رَبَّنَآ أَنزِلْ عَلَيْنَا مَآئِدَةً مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ تَكُونُ لَنَا عِيدًا لِّأَوَّلِنَا وَءَاخِرِنَا وَءَايَةً مِّنكَ ۖ وَٱرْزُقْنَا وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ ٱلرَّٰزِقِينَ (١١٤)
Said Jesus son of Mary, ‘O Allah! Our Lord! Send down to us a table from the sky, to be a festival for us, for the first ones and the last ones among us and as a sign from You, and provide for us; for You are the best of providers.’ (114)
قَالَ ٱللَّهُ إِنِّى مُنَزِّلُهَا عَلَيْكُمْ ۖ فَمَن يَكْفُرْ بَعْدُ مِنكُمْ فَإِنِّىٓ أُعَذِّبُهُۥ عَذَابًا لَّآ أُعَذِّبُهُۥٓ أَحَدًا مِّنَ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (١١٥)
Allah said, ‘I will indeed send it down to you. But should any of you disbelieve after this, I will indeed punish him with a punishment such as I do not punish anyone in all creation.’ (115)

Commentary

The Story of the Descent of the Table (al-Ma'idah) to the Disciples

Following the discussion regarding the divine favors bestowed upon Messiah (peace be upon him) and his mother in the preceding verses, these verses point to the favors granted to the Disciples (al-Hawariyyun), the close companions of Messiah (peace be upon him):

First, He says: "Remember when I inspired the Disciples to believe in Me and My Messenger [Jesus], and they accepted My call and said: 'We believe, and bear witness, O Allah, that we are Muslims and submissive to Your command'" (وَ إِذْ أَوْحَيْتُ إِلَى الْحَوارِيِّينَ أَنْ آمِنُوا بِي وَ بِرَسُولِي قالُوا آمَنَّا وَ اشْهَدْ بِأَنَّنا مُسْلِمُونَ‌).

Of course, we know that "revelation" (Wahy) in the Quran has a broad meaning and is not limited to the revelations descended upon Prophets; rather, inspirations made to the hearts of individuals are also among its instances. Therefore, regarding the mother of Moses (Surah Al-Qasas, Verse 7), the term "Wahy" [1] is used, and even regarding the instincts and innate inspirations of animals such as the honeybee, the word "Wahy" is mentioned in the Quran.

[1]: وَ أَوْحَيْنا إِلى‌ أُمِّ مُوسى‌ أَنْ أَرْضِعِيهِ فَإِذا خِفْتِ عَلَيْهِ فَأَلْقِيهِ فِي الْيَمِ‌ ...: "And We inspired the mother of Moses: 'Suckle him; but when you fear for him, cast him into the river...'"

There is also the possibility that it refers to revelations sent to them through Messiah (peace be upon him) and backed by miracles.

Regarding the Disciples—the special students and companions of Messiah (peace be upon him)—we have discussed them in Volume 2, page 426.

Then, referring to the famous event of the descent of the heavenly table, it says: "The special companions of Messiah said to Jesus (peace be upon him): 'Can your Lord send down to us a table [spread with food] from heaven?'" (إِذْ قالَ الْحَوارِيُّونَ يا عِيسَى ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ هَلْ يَسْتَطِيعُ رَبُّكَ أَنْ يُنَزِّلَ عَلَيْنا مائِدَةً مِنَ السَّماءِ).

"Ma'idah" linguistically refers to both the dining table (or tray) and the food placed upon it. It originally comes from the root "Mayd," meaning movement or shaking; perhaps the application of "Ma'idah" to the table and food is due to the movement and transfer that takes place upon it.

Messiah (peace be upon him) became concerned by this request, which carried a scent of doubt and hesitation after bringing so many other signs and miracles, and he warned them, saying: "Fear Allah, if you are [truly] believers" (قالَ اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ‌).

However, they soon informed Jesus (peace be upon him) that they had no improper motive for this proposal, and their purpose was not obstinacy, but rather: "We wish to eat from this table (and in addition to the luminosity that will arise in our hearts by consuming heavenly food—for nutrition certainly affects the human soul), that our hearts may find reassurance and tranquility, and by witnessing this great miracle, we may reach the level of 'Certainty of Sight' (Ayn al-Yaqin) and know that what you have told us was the truth, and that we may be witnesses to it" (قالُوا نُرِيدُ أَنْ نَأْكُلَ مِنْها وَ تَطْمَئِنَّ قُلُوبُنا وَ نَعْلَمَ أَنْ قَدْ صَدَقْتَنا وَ نَكُونَ عَلَيْها مِنَ الشَّاهِدِينَ‌).

When Jesus (peace be upon him) became aware of their good intentions in this request, he reflected their desire to the presence of the Lord in this manner: "O Allah, our Lord! Send down to us a table from heaven to be for us a festival (Eid) for the first of us and the last of us, and a sign from You; and provide for us, for You are the best of providers" (قالَ عِيسَى ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّنا أَنْزِلْ عَلَيْنا مائِدَةً مِنَ السَّماءِ تَكُونُ لَنا عِيداً لِأَوَّلِنا وَ آخِرِنا وَ آيَةً مِنْكَ وَ ارْزُقْنا وَ أَنْتَ خَيْرُ الرَّازِقِينَ‌).

It is noteworthy that Messiah expressed their request in a more dignified manner before Allah, reflecting a spirit of seeking truth and considering collective and public interests.

Allah accepted this prayer, which had been issued out of good intention and sincerity, and said to them: "I will indeed send it down to you; but be aware, after the descent of this table, your responsibility becomes much heavier, and after witnessing such a clear miracle, whoever among you disbelieves thereafter, I will punish him with a punishment such as I have not inflicted upon anyone in the worlds!" (قالَ اللَّهُ إِنِّي مُنَزِّلُها عَلَيْكُمْ فَمَنْ يَكْفُرْ بَعْدُ مِنْكُمْ فَإِنِّي أُعَذِّبُهُ عَذاباً لا أُعَذِّبُهُ أَحَداً مِنَ الْعالَمِينَ‌).

Key Points

In these verses, there are points that must be examined:

1- What was the purpose of the request for the table?

  • There is no doubt that the Disciples did not have an evil intention in this request, and their goal was not obstinacy against Messiah (peace be upon him); rather, they were seeking greater tranquility and the removal of remaining doubts and whispers in the depths of their hearts. For it often happens that a person proves a matter through reasoning and sometimes even experience, but because the matter is significant, remnants of doubt remain in the corners of the heart. Thus, one desires to uproot them from the depths of the heart through repeated experience or by transforming scientific reasoning into visual observation. Hence, we see that even Abraham (peace be upon him), with his high station of faith and certainty, asked Allah to see the matter of the Resurrection with his own eyes so that his scientific faith might be transformed into "Certainty of Sight" and "Witnessing."

However, since the phrasing of the Disciples' request was seemingly jarring, Jesus (peace be upon him) initially interpreted it as making excuses and objected to them. But when they clarified their objective with further explanation, Jesus (peace be upon him) yielded to them.

2- What is the meaning of "Can your Lord..." (هَلْ يَسْتَطِيعُ رَبُّكَ)?

  • Certainly, this phrase initially suggests that the Disciples doubted Allah's power to send down the table. However, Islamic commentators have provided explanations, of which a few parts seem best: First, this request was at the beginning of their journey when they were not fully acquainted with all of Allah's attributes. Second, their meaning was: "Does Allah see it as appropriate to send such a table to us?"—just as one might say to another, "I cannot give all my wealth to so-and-so," meaning "I do not see it as wise," not that he lacks the power. Third, "Yastati'" (can/is able) might mean "Yastajib" (responds/accepts), as the root "Taw'" means submission, and when it moves to the "Istif'al" form, such a meaning can be derived. Thus, the meaning of the sentence would be: "Will your Lord accept from us to send down a heavenly table?"

3- What was this heavenly table?

  • The contents of this table are not mentioned in the Quran, but from Hadiths—including a narration from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him)—it is understood that the food consisted of several loaves of bread and some fish. Perhaps the reason for requesting such a miracle was that they had heard a heavenly table (Manna and Salwa) was sent down to the Children of Israel through the miracle of Moses, so they made a similar request to Jesus.

4- Was the table actually sent down to them?

  • Although the above verses are almost explicit regarding the descent of the table—as Allah's promise is unfailing—it is strange that some commentators have doubted its descent, saying that when the Disciples felt the weight of responsibility after the table's descent, they withdrew their request! However, the truth is that the table was indeed sent down to them.

5- What is an "Eid" (Festival)?

  • "Eid" linguistically comes from the root "Awd," meaning "return." Therefore, days on which problems are removed from a people or community and they return to their initial victories and comforts are called "Eid." In Islamic festivals, because the original innate purity and clarity return to the soul and spirit in the light of obeying for one month of Ramadan or performing the great obligation of Hajj, and impurities contrary to nature are removed, it is called "Eid." Since the day of the table's descent was a day of return to victory, purity, and faith in Allah, Messiah (peace be upon him) named it "Eid." As mentioned in narrations, the descent of the table occurred on a Sunday, and perhaps one of the reasons for the sanctity of Sunday among Christians is this very event.

And if we read in a narration from Ali (peace be upon him): "Every day in which Allah is not disobeyed is a day of Eid" [1], it also refers to this same subject, for the day of abandoning sin is a day of victory, purity, and return to the original nature (Fitrah).

[1]: Nahj al-Balagha, Short Sayings, 428.

6- Why the severe punishment?

  • There is an important point here to note: when faith reaches the stage of witnessing and "Certainty of Sight"—meaning one observes the truth with their eyes and no room for doubt or whispering remains—one's responsibility becomes much heavier. Such a person is not the same as before when their faith was not based on witnessing and occasionally contained whispers; they have entered a new stage of faith and responsibility, and their slightest negligence or shortcoming will warrant a severe punishment. For this reason, the responsibility of the Prophets and the Friends of Allah (Awliya) was extremely heavy, such that they were always in awe of it. In daily life, we encounter examples of this: for instance, everyone knows that there are hungry people in their city for whom they have a responsibility, but when one sees with their own eyes an innocent person moaning from the intensity of hunger, the nature of their responsibility certainly changes.

7- The New Testament and the Table

  • In the current four Gospels, we do not find mention of the table in the form described in the Quran. Although in the Gospel of John, Chapter 21, there is a discussion about Jesus miraculously feeding a group of people with bread and fish, with a little attention, it becomes clear that it is not related to the issue of the heavenly table and the Disciples [1]. In the Book of Acts, which is among the books of the New Testament, there is also a discussion about the descent of a sheet [with food] to one of the Disciples named Peter, but that too is different from what we are discussing. However, since we know that many of the truths revealed to Jesus (peace be upon him) are not in the current Gospels—just as many things in the Gospels were not revealed to Messiah (peace be upon him)—no problem arises from this perspective regarding the event of the descent of the table 2.

[1]: Al-Huda ila Din al-Mustafa, Vol. 2, p. 239.

Ayahs

وَإِذْ قَالَ ٱللَّهُ يَـٰعِيسَى ٱبْنَ مَرْيَمَ ءَأَنتَ قُلْتَ لِلنَّاسِ ٱتَّخِذُونِى وَأُمِّىَ إِلَـٰهَيْنِ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ قَالَ سُبْحَـٰنَكَ مَا يَكُونُ لِىٓ أَنْ أَقُولَ مَا لَيْسَ لِى بِحَقٍّ ۚ إِن كُنتُ قُلْتُهُۥ فَقَدْ عَلِمْتَهُۥ ۚ تَعْلَمُ مَا فِى نَفْسِى وَلَآ أَعْلَمُ مَا فِى نَفْسِكَ ۚ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ عَلَّـٰمُ ٱلْغُيُوبِ (١١٦)
And when Allah will say, ‘O Jesus son of Mary! Was it you who said to the people, ‘‘Take me and my mother for gods besides Allah’’?’ He will say, ‘Immaculate are You! It does not behoove me to say what I have no right to [say]. Had I said it, You would certainly have known it: You know whatever is in my self, and I do not know what is in Your Self. Indeed, You are knower of all that is Unseen. (116)
مَا قُلْتُ لَهُمْ إِلَّا مَآ أَمَرْتَنِى بِهِۦٓ أَنِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ رَبِّى وَرَبَّكُمْ ۚ وَكُنتُ عَلَيْهِمْ شَهِيدًا مَّا دُمْتُ فِيهِمْ ۖ فَلَمَّا تَوَفَّيْتَنِى كُنتَ أَنتَ ٱلرَّقِيبَ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ وَأَنتَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ شَهِيدٌ (١١٧)
I did not say to them [anything] except what You had commanded me [to say]: ‘‘Worship Allah, my Lord and your Lord.’’ And I was a witness to them so long as I was among them. But when You had taken me away, You Yourself were watchful over them, and You are witness to all things. (117)
إِن تُعَذِّبْهُمْ فَإِنَّهُمْ عِبَادُكَ ۖ وَإِن تَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ فَإِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (١١٨)
If You punish them, they are indeed Your creatures; but if You forgive them, You are indeed the All-mighty, the All-wise.’ (118)

Commentary

The Disavowal of Christ from the Shirk of His Followers

These verses discuss the dialogue between Allah and Prophet Jesus (peace be upon him) on the Day of Resurrection. This is evidenced by the fact that a few verses later we read: “This is the Day when the truthful will benefit from their truthfulness” (5:119), and certainly, that day refers to the Day of Judgment.

Furthermore, the phrase “And when You took me up, You were the Watcher over them” is another proof that this conversation takes place after the period of Jesus’s (peace be upon him) prophethood. The fact that the verse begins with the word “Qala” (He said), which is in the past tense, poses no problem; it is frequently seen in the Quran that matters related to the Resurrection are mentioned in the past tense to indicate the certainty of the Resurrection. That is, its occurrence in the future is so certain that it is as if it has already happened in the past, and thus it is mentioned using the past tense.

In any case, the first verse states: On the Day of Judgment, Allah says to Jesus: “Did you say to the people, ‘Take me and my mother as two gods besides Allah’?” (وَ إِذْ قالَ اللَّهُ يا عِيسَى ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ أَ أَنْتَ قُلْتَ لِلنَّاسِ اتَّخِذُونِي وَ أُمِّي إِلهَيْنِ مِنْ دُونِ اللَّهِ).

There is no doubt that Jesus (peace be upon him) never said such a thing and only invited people to monotheism and the worship of God. However, the purpose of this interrogation is to obtain an admission from him in front of his nation and to demonstrate their condemnation.

With the utmost respect, Jesus (peace be upon him) offers several points in response to this question:

  1. First, he begins by glorifying Allah, clearing Him of any partner or likeness, saying: “O Allah! Glory be to You, [exalted are You] above having any partner” (قالَ سُبْحانَكَ).

  2. How could I possibly say that which I have no right to say? (ما يَكُونُ لِي أَنْ أَقُولَ ما لَيْسَ لِي بِحَقٍ). In fact, he not only denies saying these words but states that fundamentally he has no such right, and such speech is never compatible with his status and position.

  3. Then, he appeals to the infinite knowledge of the Lord, saying: “My witness is that if I had said it, You would have known it, for You are aware of what is within my soul and being, while I am unaware of what is in Your Holy Essence; for You are the Knower of the unseen and aware of all secrets and hidden things” (إِنْ كُنْتُ قُلْتُهُ فَقَدْ عَلِمْتَهُ تَعْلَمُ ما فِي نَفْسِي وَ لا أَعْلَمُ ما فِي نَفْسِكَ إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ عَلَّامُ الْغُيُوبِ) [1].

  4. “The only thing I said to them was what You commanded me: to invite them to Your worship and to say, ‘Worship Allah, the One God, who is my Lord and your Lord’” (ما قُلْتُ لَهُمْ إِلَّا ما أَمَرْتَنِي بِهِ أَنِ اعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ رَبِّي وَ رَبَّكُمْ).

  5. “And I was a witness over them as long as I was among them, and I did not let them take the path of polytheism. But when You took me up from among them, You were the Watcher and Guardian over them, and You are Witness over all things” (وَ كُنْتُ عَليهِمْ شَهيداً ما دُمْتُ فيهِمْ فَلَمَّا تَوَفَّيْتَني كُنْتَ أَنْتَ الرَّقيبَ عَلَيْهِمْ وَ أَنْتَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ شَهيدٌ) [2].

  6. “And despite all this, the matter is Your matter and the will is Your will. If You punish them for this great deviation, they are Your servants and are unable to escape from this punishment, and this right is established for You against Your disobedient servants. And if You forgive them and overlook their sins, You are the All-Mighty and All-Wise; neither is Your forgiveness a sign of weakness, nor is Your punishment devoid of wisdom and reckoning” (إِنْ تُعَذِّبْهُمْ فَإِنَّهُمْ عِبادُكَ وَ إِنْ تَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ فَإِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ).

[1]: Here, the application of the word Nafs (soul/self) to Allah does not mean a spirit or soul, but rather one of the meanings of Nafs is "Essence" (Dhat).

[2]: Regarding the meaning of Tawaffi and the fact that it does not mean the death of Jesus (peace be upon him), it has been discussed in detail under verse 55 of Surah Al-Imran in Volume 2, page 30.

Here, two questions arise

  1. Has it ever been seen in the history of Christians that they took "Mary" as their deity? Or is it that they only believe in the Trinity and the three gods, namely "God the Father," "God the Son," and the "Holy Spirit"? There is no doubt that the Holy Spirit, in their view, is the intermediary between "God the Father" and "God the Son," and is someone other than Mary. In response to this question, it must be said: It is true that Christians did not consider Mary to be God. However, at the same time, they performed acts of worship before her and her statue, just as idolaters did not consider the idol to be God but imagined it as a partner to God in worship. To put it more clearly, there is a difference between Allah meaning "God" and Ilah meaning "deity/object of worship." Christians considered Mary an Ilah, meaning an "object of worship," not God.

As one commentator puts it: Although none of the Christian sects apply the word "Ilah" or "deity" to Mary—rather, they only consider her the Mother of God—practically, they perform ceremonies of supplication and worship before her, whether they give her that name or not. He then adds: Some time ago in Beirut, in the ninth issue of the seventh year of the magazine "Al-Mashriq," which belongs to Christians, significant material was published commemorating the fiftieth anniversary of Pope Pius IX's declaration regarding the personality of Mary. In this same issue, it was explicitly stated that the Eastern Churches, like the Western Churches, worship Mary. In the fourteenth issue of the fifth year of the same magazine, an article by Anastas al-Karmali was published, which sought to find roots for the issue of Mary's worship even in the books of the Old Testament and the Torah, interpreting the story of the enmity between the serpent (Satan) and the woman (Eve) as referring to Mary [1]. Therefore, the worship and adoration of Mary exists among them.

  1. The other question is: How does Prophet Jesus (peace be upon him) speak about the polytheists of his nation with a phrase that carries the scent of intercession, saying: "If You forgive them, You are the All-Mighty, All-Wise"? Is a polytheist eligible for intercession and forgiveness?! In response, one must pay attention to this point: If Jesus’s (peace be upon him) goal were intercession, he should have said, "Indeed, You are the Forgiving, the Merciful," because Allah being Forgiving and Merciful is consistent with the station of intercession. However, we see that he describes God as being "All-Mighty and All-Wise." From this, it is understood that the purpose is not intercession or a request for forgiveness for them, but rather the goal is to negate any choice of his own and to entrust the matter to the will of the Lord. That is, the matter is in Your hands; if You wish, You forgive, and if You wish, You punish, although neither Your punishment is without reason nor Your forgiveness without cause, and in any case, it is beyond my power and ability.

Furthermore, it is possible that among them, a group realized their mistake and took the path of repentance, and this sentence was regarding that group.

[1]: Tafsir al-Manar, Volume 7, page 263.

Ayahs

قَالَ ٱللَّهُ هَـٰذَا يَوْمُ يَنفَعُ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ صِدْقُهُمْ ۚ لَهُمْ جَنَّـٰتٌ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَآ أَبَدًا ۚ رَّضِىَ ٱللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ وَرَضُوا۟ عَنْهُ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ (١١٩)
Allah will say, ‘This day truthfulness shall benefit the truthful. For them there will be gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them forever. Allah is pleased with them and they are pleased with Him. That is the great success.’ (119)
لِلَّهِ مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا فِيهِنَّ ۚ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌۢ (١٢٠)
To Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and whatever there is in them, and He has power over all things. (120)

Commentary

The Great Success

Following the mention of the dialogue between Allah and Christ عليه السلام on the Day of Resurrection, which was detailed in the preceding verses, we read in this verse: After this dialogue, Allah declares: "This is the Day when the truthful will benefit from their truthfulness" (قالَ اللَّهُ هذا يَوْمُ يَنْفَعُ الصَّادِقِينَ صِدْقُهُمْ‌).

Of course, the intent of this phrase is truthfulness and sincerity in speech and action during this world, which becomes beneficial in the Hereafter; otherwise, truthfulness in the Hereafter—which is not a place of religious obligation—would be of no benefit. Furthermore, the circumstances of that Day are such that no one can speak anything but the truth, and even the sinners and wrongdoers will all confess to their evil deeds; thus, there is no room for lying on that Day.

Therefore, those who fulfilled their responsibilities and missions and walked only the path of truth and integrity—such as Christ عليه السلام and his true followers, or the true followers of other Prophets who entered through the door of sincerity in this world—will reap the full benefit of their work.

Additionally, it can be inferred from this phrase that all virtues can be summarized under the title of "truthfulness": truthfulness in speech and truthfulness in action. On the Day of Resurrection, the only capital that is of use is truthfulness and sincerity, and nothing else.

He then describes the reward of the truthful: "For them are gardens [in Paradise] beneath which rivers flow, wherein they will abide forever" (لَهُمْ جَنَّاتٌ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها أَبَداً).

And more important than this material blessing is that "Allah is pleased with them and they are pleased with Him" (رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ وَ رَضُوا عَنْهُ‌).

"And there is no doubt that this great bounty, which combines both material and spiritual blessings, is considered the great success" (ذلِكَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ‌).

It is noteworthy that in this verse, after mentioning the gardens of Paradise with all their blessings, He mentions the blessing of Allah’s pleasure with His servants and the servants’ pleasure with Allah, followed by "ذلِكَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ‌" (That is the great success). This demonstrates the immense importance of this mutual satisfaction (the Lord’s pleasure with the servants and the servants’ pleasure with the Lord). For it is possible for a person to be immersed in the highest blessings, yet if he feels that his Master, his Deity, and his Beloved is displeased with him, all those blessings and bounties will turn bitter in his soul.

Likewise, it is possible for a person to possess everything but not be satisfied or content with what he has. Clearly, all those blessings will not make him happy with such a mindset; a mysterious distress will constantly torment him and deprive him of peace of mind and soul, which is the greatest divine bounty.

Furthermore, when Allah is pleased with someone, He grants them whatever they desire, and when He grants them whatever they desire, they in turn become pleased. The conclusion is that the highest blessing is for Allah to be pleased with man and for man to be satisfied with his God.

In the final verse, reference is made to Allah’s ownership and sovereignty over the heavens and the earth and whatever is within them, and the universality of His power over all things is stated: "To Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and whatever is within them. And He is over all things competent" (لِلَّهِ مُلْكُ السَّماواتِ وَ الْأَرْضِ وَ ما فِيهِنَّ وَ هُوَ عَلى‌ كُلِّ شَيْ‌ءٍ قَدِيرٌ).

The mention of this phrase serves as a reason and cause for the servants' satisfaction with Allah; for He who is powerful over all things and governs the entire universe has the ability to grant His servants whatever they ask of Him and make them content and satisfied.

Additionally, it could be an allusion to the incorrectness of the Christians' action in worshipping Mary, as worship is only worthy of Him who governs the entire creation, not Mary, who was nothing more than a creature.

(End of Surah Al-Ma'idah)
